description_long: This is a large, minimalist desk structure composed of four thick, flat, rectangular panels arranged perpendicularly to form two work cubicles with high privacy walls. The panels are a dark, matte color, likely made of wood or laminate, and extend both vertically and horizontally to create a semi-enclosed workspace on each side. The central divider runs the full length of the desk, while two shorter side panels create individual compartments. There are no visible drawers, handles, or other interactive components, emphasizing a clean and functional design. The desk appears robust and heavy, suitable for high-traffic or public environments. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended for shared or public workspaces such as libraries, study halls, or office hot-desking areas.
description: A large, dark, multi-panel desk with two high-walled cubicles, designed for privacy and shared workspace environments. The structure is robust, minimalist, and lacks visible storage or interactive features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two cubicle openings and the central divider, with a clear view into one workspace.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the desk and the thickness of the panels, with both cubicles visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the solid back panels and the closed-off nature of the cubicles, with no entry points visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the length and depth of the desk and the arrangement of the dividers.
attribute_orientation: The open cubicle sides are intended to face the user, with the central divider providing privacy; the solid back panel marks the rear.
attribute_usage_context: study cubicle or shared workspace desk
